div.h3: Required for pivot_root() in Linux Container Runtimes

div.p: The pivot_root syscall requires that the target new_root is a mount point. Normal directories in a rootfs do not have their own vfsmount entries in the kernel.
A bind-mount-to-self (mount --bind /dir /dir) forces the kernel to:

ol.li: Allocate a struct mount object for that path.
Satisfy the VFS check path_has_submounts() or equivalent mount-point checks.

div.p: This is the standard pattern used by runc and containerd during the setup of a container's isolated root.
Status: pivot_root() failure pivot_root("/newroot", ...) Returns: EINVAL Reason: Path is not a mount point.
Status: pivot_root() Ready VFS node now associated with a vfsmount struct.

ol.li: Is new_root a mount point? (0xBBBB2222 -> YES)
Does new_root have a parent? (root / -> YES)
